name: Goodyear San Angelo
description_short: Goodyear San Angelo Federal Credit Union is a federally chartered, NCUA-insured credit union based in San Angelo, TX, offering savings, CDs, mortgages, loans, and other banking products.
description_long:
Goodyear San Angelo Federal Credit Union was established in 1974 and operates as a federally chartered, NCUA-insured institution headquartered in San Angelo, Texas. The credit union serves its local membership with traditional credit union banking services and products. As of 2026, the institution holds approximately $3.66 million in assets, $3.13 million in loans, $3.11 million in deposits, and $0.55 million in capital, indicating a small, community-focused credit union.

The institution offers a comprehensive suite of banking products including savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs) with terms ranging from 1 to 5 years, mortgage loans (both 15-year and 30-year fixed options), home equity lines of credit (HELOC), auto loans, personal loans, and other standard credit union services. Their advertised rates as of April 2026 include 4.20% APY on savings, 4.59% on 1-year CDs, 4.00% on 2 and 5-year CDs, 4.91% on 30-year mortgages, 4.39% on 15-year mortgages, and 0.99% on HELOCs.

As a credit union rather than a traditional bank, Goodyear San Angelo operates on a member-owned, not-for-profit basis, which theoretically allows it to offer more competitive rates and lower fees than commercial banks. Its small asset base and local focus suggest it serves primarily San Angelo-area residents and businesses. The institution maintains NCUA deposit insurance protection, which provides standard coverage up to $250,000 per account category.

A significant limitation is that the BestCashCow profile explicitly states "We do not yet have rates for Goodyear San Angelo" for both deposit and loan products as of the data collection date, making current rate comparisons impossible through this source. Prospective members would need to contact the institution directly to obtain accurate current rate information and terms. The small size of the institution and limited online presence suggest it operates primarily as a local, in-person banking option with less sophisticated digital banking infrastructure compared to larger regional or national credit unions.
pros: NCUA federally insured deposits with standard $250,000 per category protection
Credit union member-owned structure typically offers lower fees and more competitive rates than commercial banks
Long operational history since 1974 demonstrates institutional stability and community presence
Advertised rates competitive for 2026: 4.59% on 1-year CDs, 4.91% on 30-year mortgages
Offers comprehensive product suite including savings, CDs, mortgages, HELOCs, auto and personal loans
Local San Angelo headquarters provides personalized, community-focused banking service
cons: Current rates and terms unavailable through major comparison sources; requires direct contact with institution
Very small asset base ($3.66M) limits lending capacity and product sophistication compared to larger credit unions
Likely limited digital banking infrastructure and online account management capabilities due to small size
No indication of mobile banking app, online loan applications, or remote account opening services
Geographic limitation as local San Angelo institution may lack branch network or service availability outside immediate area
best_for: San Angelo, Texas area residents seeking local, community-based banking with personal service
Credit union members prioritizing NCUA insurance protection and member-owned organizational structure
Borrowers with established relationships in the local community seeking mortgages or personal loans
Individuals seeking to avoid large commercial banks and support local financial institutions
diagnosis: Goodyear San Angelo Federal Credit Union is best suited for San Angelo-area residents and businesses seeking local, member-owned banking with NCUA federal insurance protection. The primary caveat is that current rates and loan terms are not available through standard comparison sources, requiring direct contact with the institution, and its very small asset base ($3.66M) may limit product availability and digital banking capabilities compared to larger regional credit unions.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
